content:
🚀 This iteration integrates a structured decision economy, automatic consequences for repeated mistakes, multifaceted hidden skill checks, and a resurrection system tied to literary “exile” instead of character death.

📜 1. Decision Economy – Structuring Player Choices Without Overload

📜 To prevent decision fatigue, each player has a limited number of actions per week.

✔ Every player may take ONE major decision per week.
✔ Players must wait their turn before making another move unless they spend XP to act again.
✔ Some decisions lock other choices out (e.g., committing to one faction prevents betraying another).

📜 Spending XP to Buy More Actions
✔ Players may spend XP to “buy” a second move, but it becomes progressively more expensive each time.
context: From document: RPG Character System (Fully Integrated into Weekly Modules) — chunk 287 (chunk 287/555)
